text.title: W3C Design Tokens Standard Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Component CSS references `var(--color-blue-500)` directly; rebrand requires touching every component.
antipattern.fix: Force semantic tokens for any component-facing variable; primitives only inside the semantic layer.

antipattern.symptom: Build emits empty value for `{color.brand-primary}`; UI shows transparent text on transparent.
antipattern.fix: Run alias resolver before publish; fail build if any alias unresolved.

antipattern.symptom: Token list contains `color-primary-ios`; iOS renames break web.
antipattern.root-cause: Author migrated platform-specific files without abstracting names.
antipattern.fix: Strip suffixes; move platform difference to platform-specific build config.

antipattern.symptom: Semantic token `text-on-brand` pairs `gray-400` text on `blue-500` background; contrast 2.8:1.
antipattern.root-cause: Contrast check skipped during token authoring.
antipattern.fix: Run contrast validator over every foreground/background semantic pair; fail at <4.5:1.
